prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(report_incident pump_1)
(allocate_contingency_resource_to_site pump_1 contingency_pump_1)
(assign_diagnostic_tool_to_site pump_1 flow_meter_1)
(confirm_diagnosis_for_site pump_1 flow_meter_1)
(assign_specialist_team_to_site pump_1 electrical_repair_team)
(stage_repair_location pump_1 pump_discharge_port_1 flow_meter_1)
(deploy_specialist_team_for_containment pump_1 pump_discharge_port_1 electrical_repair_team)
(release_specialist_team_from_site pump_1 electrical_repair_team)
(return_diagnostic_tool_to_inventory pump_1 flow_meter_1)
(report_incident sector_1)
(allocate_contingency_resource_to_site sector_1 contingency_pump_2)
(assign_diagnostic_tool_to_site sector_1 flow_meter_1)
(confirm_diagnosis_for_site sector_1 flow_meter_1)
(assign_specialist_team_to_site sector_1 electrical_repair_team)

(release_specialist_team_from_site sector_1 electrical_repair_team)
(return_diagnostic_tool_to_inventory sector_1 flow_meter_1)
(assemble_deployment_package pump_1 sector_1 pump_discharge_port_1 temporary_bypass_pump_1 deployment_package_alpha)
(assign_diagnostic_tool_to_site pump_1 flow_meter_1)
(mobilize_deployment_package_crew deployment_package_alpha pump_1 flow_meter_1)
(mark_component_repaired pump_1 deployment_package_alpha)
(mark_sector_repaired sector_1 deployment_package_alpha)
(authorize_restoration_and_assign_salvage pump_1 reduced_flow_salvage_plan_1 flow_meter_1)
(complete_restoration_and_return_resource pump_1 contingency_pump_1 reduced_flow_salvage_plan_1)
(return_diagnostic_tool_to_inventory pump_1 flow_meter_1)
(assign_diagnostic_tool_to_site sector_1 flow_meter_1)
(authorize_restoration_and_assign_salvage sector_1 reduced_flow_salvage_plan_1 flow_meter_1)
(complete_restoration_on_sector_and_return_resource sector_1 contingency_pump_2 reduced_flow_salvage_plan_1)
(return_diagnostic_tool_to_inventory sector_1 flow_meter_1)
(report_incident work_order_1)
(allocate_contingency_resource_to_site work_order_1 contingency_pump_1)
(assign_diagnostic_tool_to_site work_order_1 flow_meter_1)
(confirm_diagnosis_for_site work_order_1 flow_meter_1)
(authorize_contractor_for_work_order work_order_1 contractor_excavation_1 flow_meter_1)
(assign_specialist_team_to_site work_order_1 electrical_repair_team)
(perform_contractor_onboarding_checks work_order_1 electrical_repair_team)
(assign_certification_to_work_order work_order_1 safety_certification_level_1)
(validate_contractor_certification work_order_1 safety_certification_level_1)
(release_certification_from_work_order work_order_1 safety_certification_level_1)
(release_specialist_team_from_site work_order_1 electrical_repair_team)
(close_work_order_after_contractor_validation work_order_1)
(authorize_restoration_and_assign_salvage work_order_1 reduced_flow_salvage_plan_1 flow_meter_1)
(complete_restoration_on_work_order_and_return_resource work_order_1 contingency_pump_1 reduced_flow_salvage_plan_1)
(return_diagnostic_tool_to_inventory work_order_1 flow_meter_1)
(report_incident work_order_2)
(allocate_contingency_resource_to_site work_order_2 contingency_pump_2)
(assign_diagnostic_tool_to_site work_order_2 flow_meter_1)
(confirm_diagnosis_for_site work_order_2 flow_meter_1)
(reserve_and_allocate_replacement_part work_order_2 control_panel_module deployment_package_alpha)
(validate_and_assign_equipment_to_work_order work_order_2 control_panel_module deployment_package_alpha flow_meter_1)
(assign_equipment_to_work_order work_order_2 rental_generator_1)
(assign_specialist_team_to_site work_order_2 electrical_repair_team)
(perform_initial_equipment_validation work_order_2 rental_generator_1 electrical_repair_team control_panel_module deployment_package_alpha)
(assign_certification_to_work_order work_order_2 safety_certification_level_1)
(perform_higher_level_validation work_order_2 safety_certification_level_1 control_panel_module deployment_package_alpha)
(close_work_order_and_record_completion work_order_2)
(authorize_restoration_and_assign_salvage work_order_2 reduced_flow_salvage_plan_1 flow_meter_1)
(complete_restoration_on_work_order_and_return_resource work_order_2 contingency_pump_2 reduced_flow_salvage_plan_1)
message.reasoning:
The problem is an irrigation system failure recovery scenario. The goal is to achieve restoration_completed_for_target for pump_1, sector_1, work_order_1, and work_order_2.


## Examining Problem Context  
- The task focuses on recovering from an irrigation system failure.  
- The goal is to ensure restoration is completed for pump_1, sector_1, and work orders work_order_1 and work_order_2.

## Examining Problem Context  
- The task involves recovering from an irrigation system failure, ensuring restoration for pump_1, sector_1, and work orders work_order_1 and work_order_2.  
- Pump_1 is an infrastructure_component, sector_1 is an irrigation_sector, and both work orders are subsystem_subtype, all classified under incident_site in the type hierarchy.  

## Analyzing Incident Reporting  
- Incident reporting is the first step, with pump_1, sector_1, work_order_1, and work_order_2 all eligible as incident_site.  
- The process includes diagnosing, assigning specialists, and managing replacements, permits, and funding for restoration.  

## Exploring Work Order Details  
- Work_order_1 and work_order_2 both target pump_1 and sector_1, assigned to deployment_package_alpha with different replacement parts.  
- Contractors contractor_excavation_1 and contractor_electrical_2 are available and already assigned to work_order_1 and work_order_2, respectively.

## Exploring Resource Limitations  
- Achieving restoration for pump_1, sector_1, and work orders requires managing limited resources like contingency (three available) and salvage (one available).  
- Actions must be sequenced or shared to ensure all goals are met, given constraints like diagnostic tools and specialists assigned to specific sites.  

## Planning Incident Recovery  
- Starting with reporting an incident for pump_1 sets the stage for further actions like allocating contingency and diagnosing.  
- For pump_1, containment involves staging a repair location and deploying specialist teams, requiring diagnosis confirmation first.  

## Analyzing Containment Options  
- Pump_1 (infrastructure_component) uses actions like stage_repair_location for containment, while sector_1 (irrigation_sector) requires staging temporary systems.  
- Both require specialist teams and consumable kits, with effects like repair_location_staged or temporary_system_option_staged setting up for deployment.  

## Outlining Deployment Steps  
- Assembling deployment packages requires both component and sector to be contained and ready for repair, with mobilized crews and staged locations.  
- Work orders, already assigned to deployment_package_alpha, need actions like reserving replacement parts, ensuring diagnosis and deployment readiness.

## Managing Diagnostic Tools  
- Diagnostic tools, like flow_meter_1, must be assigned to specific sites for diagnosis, but can be reassigned after returning to inventory.  
- For pump_1, the tool needs to be returned from sector_1 first, then reassigned to enable crew mobilization.  

## Planning Contingency Allocation  
- Three contingency resources are available, but four sites (pump_1, sector_1, work_order_1, work_order_2) need allocation for diagnosis and containment.  
- Allocating to pump_1 and sector_1 first leaves no resources for work_order_2, creating a resource bottleneck for full restoration.  

## Sequencing Work Order Actions  
- Work orders require incident reporting, contingency allocation, and diagnosis confirmation, similar to components and sectors.  
- Without additional contingency, work_order_2 cannot progress past triage, stalling diagnosis and restoration efforts.  

## Evaluating Resource Sharing  
- Contingency resources are site-specific and cannot be shared, limiting options for sequencing actions.  
- Exploring alternatives, like reporting incidents without contingency, is blocked by diagnosis requirements for containment.

## Reviewing Restoration Process  
- Restoring pump_1 requires marking it repaired, which needs both pump_1 and sector_1 to be contained first.  
- Assembling deployment packages mandates both component_contained and sector_contained, blocking early restoration of one site.  

## Assessing Contingency Constraints  
- Three contingency resources are insufficient for four sites, including pump_1, sector_1, and two work orders.  
- Work orders need contingency for diagnosis, with no alternative path to confirmation without triage assignment.  

## Exploring Work Order Closure  
- Restoring work orders involves a long chain starting from diagnosis confirmation, with no shortcuts identified.  
- Actions like closing work orders still require validated high-level checks, tied to initial equipment assessments.

## Examining Contingency Requirements  
- All paths to repair pump_1 and sector_1 require diagnosis confirmation, which needs contingency allocation for triage, totaling four sites needing resources.  
- Only three contingencies are available, creating a resource gap with no workaround, as contingencies cannot be shared across sites.  

## Investigating Work Order Paths  
- Work orders, like pump_1 and sector_1, also need diagnosis confirmation for repair, with no alternative paths identified to bypass contingency allocation.  
- Contractor authorization for work orders still requires diagnosis confirmation, reinforcing the need for additional contingency resources.  

## Analyzing Repair Dependencies  
- Repairing components like pump_1 and sectors requires both to be contained, which depends on staging deployment packages and mobilizing crews.  
- All deployment package assembly actions mandate both component and sector containment, with no variants allowing early repair of one site.

## Investigating Contingency Allocation  
- All containment actions for pump_1 and sector_1 require diagnosis confirmation, needing two contingency allocations.  
- Work orders also need diagnosis, adding two more, totaling four contingencies, but only three are available, leaving a gap.  

## Exploring Work Order Restoration  
- Closing work orders requires repair completion, tied to diagnosis, with no shortcuts identified.  
- Marking pump_1 and sector_1 repaired needs both contained and crew mobilized, still requiring contingency for diagnosis.  

## Analyzing Resource Recovery  
- Restoring pump_1 and sector_1 could free contingencies if completed before work orders, using returned resources for wos.  
- Authorizing restoration needs salvage and diagnostic tools, but only one salvage is available, limiting options for sector_1.

## Prioritizing Repair Sequences  
- Completing repairs for pump_1 and sector_1 first allows marking them repaired without work order involvement, using mark_component_repaired and mark_sector_repaired after staging deployment packages and mobilizing crews.  
- This approach enables authorizing and completing restoration for pump_1 and sector_1, freeing up two contingencies and salvage for later use on work orders.  

## Evaluating Work Order Processes  
- Work orders require diagnosis confirmation for closure, with no bypass identified, such as through contractor paths, which still need validated checks like diagnosis_confirmed.  
- Closing work orders involves contractor onboarding and certification validation, all tied to initial diagnosis, but can proceed once contingencies are freed from earlier repairs.

## Detailing Repair Execution  
- Initial steps include reporting incidents for pump_1 and sector_1, allocating contingencies, and assigning diagnostic tools.  
- Diagnosis confirmation for both sites involves specialist teams, staging repair locations, and deploying teams for containment.  

- Specialist teams are released after containment, freeing resources for other tasks.  
- Diagnostic tools are returned to inventory after use, ensuring availability for subsequent steps.  

## Staging Deployment Packages  
- Assemble deployment package alpha for pump_1 and sector_1, involving repair locations and temporary systems.  
- Mobilize deployment package crew with diagnostic tools assigned, enabling marking components and sectors as repaired.  

## Completing Restoration Process  
- Authorize and complete restoration for pump_1, freeing contingency_pump_1 and salvage resources.  
- Repeat the process for sector_1, ensuring contingency_pump_2 and salvage are available, meeting all repair goals.

## Exploring Work Order Closures  
- Closing work orders without contractors is possible using close_work_order_and_record_completion, requiring validated_high_level status.  
- This approach avoids cleared_for_execution and equipment execution, focusing on high-level validation for closure.  

## Investigating Validation Paths  
- Validated_high_level status comes from perform_higher_level_validation, needing equipment_initial_check_complete and certification.  
- Equipment checks require work orders to have assigned, validated equipment, with resources allocated but not mobilized.  

## Examining Funding and Execution  
- Starting work order execution needs validated_high_level, cleared_for_execution, and funding, creating a dependency loop.  
- Funding assignment requires validated_high_level and cleared_for_execution, suggesting validation must precede funding allocation.

## Assessing Work Order Paths  
- For work_order_1, a contractor is available, allowing the contractor path for repair execution.  
- For work_order_2, no contractor is available, so explore equipment-based paths instead of contractor reliance.  

## Exploring Equipment Alternatives  
- Identified rental_generator_1 and control_panel_module as available equipment for work_order_2, with deployment_package_alpha already staged.  
- Crew mobilization is confirmed, enabling use of equipment validation without needing contractor involvement.  

## Validating Equipment Assignment  
- Reserve and allocate control_panel_module for work_order_2, ensuring diagnosis is confirmed and replacement part is available.  
- Use validate_and_assign_equipment_to_work_order to set equipment_assigned_validated for work_order_2, as deployment_package_alpha remains unmobilized.

## Confirming Work Order Paths  
- For work_order_1, use contractor path since contractor_electrical_2 is available, avoiding equipment or replacement part reserves.  
- For work_order_2, with no contractor, use equipment path requiring replacement part reserve, equipment, certification, and specialist.  

## Checking Equipment Path Preconditions  
- Reserve control_panel_module for work_order_2, needing deployment_package_alpha staged and crew mobilized, both confirmed.  
- Validate equipment assignment requires unmobilized deployment_package_alpha, satisfied since assemble_deployment_package was used without setting mobilized.  

## Sequencing Equipment Validation Steps  
- Assign rental_generator_1 and electrical_repair_team to work_order_2, then perform initial equipment validation, setting initial_check.  
- Complete with certification and higher-level validation, ensuring work_order_2 reaches validated_high_level for closure, all without mobilization.

## Continuing Equipment Path Validation  
- For work_order_2, report incident and allocate contingency_pump_2 to site, ensuring resources are ready.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
